theme: regulator, court, enforcement, and company-notice leads
goal: Promote auth:LIMEN-000005 from document-presence support to exact caption/case-number normalization using only the already captured public DOJ indictment PDF and bounded local OCR.

preferred_route.toolchain: marker-pdf or equivalent bounded OCR stack in an isolated environment
preferred_route.reason: Host has ample free disk and can rasterize the PDF, but no ready OCR binary or OCR Python stack is installed.
preferred_route.network_need: package/model installation may require network once; OCR itself should run on the already captured local PDF

expected_outputs: exact caption string from the document body if legible
exact case number from the document body if legible
patch-ready field updates for authoritative ledgers and court-family matrices
explicit failure note if OCR remains too low-confidence to promote fields

safe_resource_limits.network: package/model setup only if needed; no new evidence crawling

paper_thesis_use: Methods appendix note distinguishing access barriers from toolchain barriers inside U.S. court/public-record work.
Dashboard facet for host OCR readiness versus source availability.
Thesis reproducibility note showing when exact court-anchor normalization is blocked by local tooling rather than by public-source absence.
success_condition: At least one exact caption or case-number field becomes legible enough to patch shared ledgers, or OCR failure is reproduced with confidence metadata strong enough to keep the blocker class explicit.
boundary_notes: Use only the already captured local DOJ PDF for OCR; no PACER login, fees, sealed records, or private attachments.
Do not promote charging-stage allegations into adjudicated facts.
If OCR output is ambiguous, retain the current blocker instead of guessing fields.
